Magnus and Ratatosk should remain backend capability modules rather than separate user-facing chat profiles.
Public posting, email send, calendar edit/delete, Gmail cleanup, Monarch mutations, and broker orders require explicit policy gates and canaries.

Torben SOUL.md defines one Signal-facing COO operator with hidden EA, GTM, and Finance scopes.

Current Ratatosk live trading remains blocked by live consent, live env gates, circuit breaker, and trading halt.

unsupported.claim: No active Hermes ratatosk profile exists on this host.

supported_claims: Magnus can remain GTM submanager behind Torben.
Ratatosk can remain Finance submanager behind Torben.
unsupported_claims: All remaining backend capabilities are production-ready.
LinkedIn/X posting is ready for unattended automation.
contradicted_claims: Static crons should decide what matters without LLM review.
needs_clarification: Whether Eric wants public posting enabled after approval only, or later unattended under hard caps.
Whether Ratatosk should become a separate hidden Hermes profile or remain a Torben-owned script package.
Whether email send and calendar edit/delete should stay approval-only beyond the first 30 days.

section_policy.reason: The remaining scope includes email send, calendar edit/delete, public posting, financial data, and broker orders.
Torben must prove submanager health, canaries, dedupe, and debug loops.
The main risk is unsafe mutation under a single conversational operator.
End-to-end profile, Signal, API, canary, and fail-closed tests are required.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: brief missing or schema-invalid before this planning slice

verification_spec.scope_note: Validate the decomposition artifact before turning it into a /goal execution loop.

enterprise_readiness_contract.production_grade_target: Torben is the only user-facing Signal operator and can safely coordinate EA, GTM, and Finance backend capabilities with approval-gated mutations, canaries, debug loops, and clear submanager ownership.
enterprise_readiness_contract.backward_compatibility: Preserve current Torben EA/GTM jobs, action handles, Signal reply routing, Gmail Pub/Sub realtime triage, calendar busy-block alignment, and no-noise cron policy.
enterprise_readiness_contract.forward_compatibility: Allow Magnus and Ratatosk to stay as hidden submanagers behind Torben, with typed capability contracts that can later be replaced without changing the Signal surface.
failure_mode_coverage: Submanager output bypasses Torben and creates multiple user-facing chat surfaces.
A cron posts, sends, edits, deletes, or trades without an action-ledger approval.
A public-write or broker-order path succeeds without audit evidence and rollback/reconciliation metadata.
Successful maintenance jobs create Signal noise instead of staying silent.
A live-profile failure repeats alerts without investigation and dedupe.
definition_of_done: Torben owns the EA/GTM/Finance action ledger and submanager health view.
Magnus GTM and Grok/X algorithm capabilities run behind Torben with draft/source/hold reply loops and public_actions_taken=0 until approval.
Ratatosk finance capabilities run behind Torben with research, staging, mandate, consent, guard, reconciliation, and fail-closed live order checks.
Email send, calendar edit/delete, Gmail cleanup, X/LinkedIn posting, Monarch mutation, and broker orders have explicit approval gates, canaries, and audit artifacts before enablement.
The validation task proves happy path, failure path, no-noise path, duplicate suppression, and live-profile LLM investigation behavior.
enterprise_readiness_contract.compliance_posture: High-risk personal automation. Treat email, calendar, finance, public posting, and trading as approval-gated external mutations with least-privilege OAuth/MCP tokens, audit logs, and revocation paths.
paved_road_refs: Google Gmail API users.messages.send and users.messages.trash
Google Calendar API events.insert and events.patch/delete
X API v2 create post endpoint
production_invariant_coverage: external_mutations=0 unless explicit action-ledger approval exists
public_actions_taken=0 unless explicit public-write approval exists
broker_orders_submitted=0 unless live finance decision, mandate, consent, kill-switch, active halt state, and guard pass
every visible action has EA/GTM/FIN handle and evidence links

sections.1_context: Torben is now the active single Signal-facing COO operator. The current live profile owns the morning brief, calendar alignment, meeting prep, Gmail Pub/Sub realtime triage, Gmail watch renewal, Boardy brief, email hygiene recommendations, GTM radar, GTM engagement radar, live-profile verification, and live-profile LLM investigation. The old magnus Hermes profile has no scheduled jobs, while Torben calls Magnus-backed GTM logic and writes GTM handles. Ratatosk has Robinhood v0.1 repo work and a passing v0.1 validator, but there is no active Hermes ratatosk profile and live trading remains blocked.
sections.2_scope_and_constraints: Scope: finish backend capabilities so Torben can coordinate EA, GTM, and Finance through one Signal thread. Keep Magnus as hidden GTM submanager if useful; keep Ratatosk as hidden Finance submanager if useful. Constraints: no OpenClaw dependency, no duplicate user-facing agents, no static cron decision-making, no unattended public posting/sending/deleting/trading, no secrets in repo/logs, no clearing finance halts or live gates inside the implementation goal without separate current risk evidence and explicit approval.
sections.3_repo_findings: Hermes/Torben: active cron jobs prove Torben is the live operator and profile verification is green. Torben SOUL.md defines the intended one-operator pattern and mutation boundaries. GTM artifacts show Grok/xAI judging, X algorithm lens from xai-org/x-algorithm, action handles, and public_actions_taken=0. Magnus: content intelligence exists and can produce article recommendations, GTM signals, and draft/source/hold reply loops, but the magnus Hermes profile itself is empty on this host. Ratatosk: docs/build-briefs/hermes-robinhood-cron-v0_1.json and scripts/robinhood_v01_validate.py show staged trading workflow readiness. The testing-scope live finance decision is approved, but a consent/env preflight still blocks live trading on the active circuit breaker/trading halt.
sections.4_architecture: Torben remains the facade and policy owner. Backend submanagers expose typed capabilities: EA, GTM/Magnus, and Finance/Ratatosk. Each capability must produce EvidencePacket, CandidateAction, ApprovalRequest, ExecutionReport, and ReconciliationReport artifacts. Torben owns dedupe, action handles, Signal copy, approval resolution, live-profile health, and failure investigation. Submanagers own source collection, domain scoring, draft generation, adapters, and reconciliation, but may not talk directly to Eric or mutate externally without Torben policy clearance.

5_security_review.risk: Public posting, email sending, calendar edits, Gmail cleanup, Monarch writes, and broker orders are external mutations.
5_security_review.control: Require action-ledger approval, policy guard, provider response capture, and rollback or reconciliation metadata before any mutation.

5_security_review.risk: LLM prompted by untrusted email, calendar, social, or market inputs could call unsafe tools.
5_security_review.control: Keep LLMs on bounded JSON contracts; tool adapters enforce action classes independent of model output.

5_security_review.risk: Finance live trading can lose money quickly, especially margin/options.
5_security_review.control: Keep live trading blocked until mandate, consent, limits, circuit breaker, kill switch, account discovery, and reconciliation pass.

5_security_review.risk: Secrets and OAuth tokens can leak through logs or repo files.
5_security_review.control: Use OAuth or MCP token stores and 1Password/runtime injection where applicable; never print secret values.

sections.6_observability_slo: Torben backend completion must expose: per-submanager health, last successful source refresh, last LLM decision status, dedupe counts, pending action handles, external_mutations count, public_actions_taken count, broker_orders_submitted count, delivery status, and live-profile investigation state. SLO: healthy background no-op jobs stay silent; actionable failures alert once per fingerprint and create an investigation request; duplicate failures suppress repeat Signal noise.
sections.7_execution_plan: Phase A: normalize Torben submanager contracts and live-profile health for EA/GTM/Finance. Phase B: finish Magnus as hidden GTM submanager with source refresh, Grok/X algorithm lens, reply routing, content packages, and approval-only X/LinkedIn publishing adapters. Phase C: finish EA mutation backends for Gmail send, Gmail cleanup, and real calendar edits/deletes behind explicit approvals. Phase D: finish Ratatosk as hidden Finance submanager with Robinhood and Monarch adapters, mandate/consent, research candidates, staged reviews, and fail-closed live gates. Phase E: run full canary suite: no-noise, failure investigation, approval apply, public-write dry-run, finance stage-only, live-order-blocked, and rollback/reconciliation.

8_task_tickets.title: Lock Torben single-operator backend completion scope
8_task_tickets.objective: Define the target architecture where Torben owns the Signal interface and policy surface while Magnus and Ratatosk operate as hidden backend submanagers.

decision_contract.resolution: Torben is the user-facing operator; submanagers are hidden capability modules.

scope: Hidden EA, GTM/Magnus, and Finance/Ratatosk capability boundaries
out_of_scope: Separate user-facing Magnus or Ratatosk chat profiles
acceptance_criteria: Torben is documented as the only user-facing operator.
Magnus and Ratatosk backend roles are documented as hidden submanager contracts.
All remaining mutation classes are mapped to approval gates.
anti_slop_rules: Do not describe submanagers as separate people Eric must talk to.
Do not call draft/stage readiness production mutation readiness.
Do not create a scope that depends on OpenClaw.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Existing old profile artifacts may remain on disk.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Physical deletion of old stacks can happen after Torben contracts are proven.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Live-profile verification must ignore retired profiles and check only active Torben-owned jobs.

compatibility_contract.backward: Do not break current Torben EA/GTM cron jobs or Signal replies.
compatibility_contract.forward: Allow additional submanager capabilities without changing the user-facing interface.

implementation_notes: Update Torben operating docs and build goal before code changes.

verification_spec.scope_note: Validate against live Torben profile state.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture live Torben cron list and live-profile verifier artifact.
definition_of_done: Single-operator scope is explicit and referenced by the goal.
failure_modes: HIGH: Team implements backend capability as a new user-facing profile instead of Torben-owned module.

8_task_tickets.title: Decide public and personal external mutation policy
8_task_tickets.objective: Resolve whether the next build may enable approval-gated public posting, email sending, real calendar edits/deletes, and Gmail cleanup mutations.

decision_contract.resolution: ADLC planning gate remains unresolved by schema; execution-time testing approval is recorded in docs/operations/torben-backend-capability-canary-report.md.

scope: X and LinkedIn post/reply/schedule approval policy
Gmail send and cleanup approval policy
acceptance_criteria: Each public/personal mutation class is set to blocked, approval-only, or later-autonomy.
The approval command syntax is documented for each enabled class.
The rollback/reconciliation requirement is documented for each enabled class.
anti_slop_rules: Do not bundle all mutation classes under one vague yes.
Do not enable browser automation as a fallback without audit evidence.
Do not use a cron success message as approval.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Current Gmail cleanup and GTM package paths are approval-gated but not all public/personal mutations exist.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Later autonomy can be added after 30 days of approval telemetry.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Testing autonomy does not permit unattended public writes; adapters must still prove action-ledger approval and provider results.

compatibility_contract.forward: Mutation policy can be widened later without changing evidence contracts.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Start with approval-only canaries before any autonomy.

implementation_notes: Use this as the gate for public write adapters and personal mutation resolvers.
evidence_responsibilities: Record Eric's decision in the goal closeout and action policy.
definition_of_done: Decision resolved before public/personal mutation adapters are enabled.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Public post, email send, calendar edit, or Gmail delete happens without explicit approval.

8_task_tickets.title: Decide live finance and trading mandate
8_task_tickets.objective: Resolve whether Ratatosk/Torben may enable approval-gated Robinhood live trading, margin/options, Monarch finance actions, and broker order execution under a written mandate.

decision_contract.resolution: ADLC planning gate remains unresolved by schema; execution-time testing approval is recorded in docs/operations/torben-backend-capability-canary-report.md.

scope: Robinhood Agentic MCP live order policy
Margin and options eligibility and caps
out_of_scope: Clearing the circuit breaker without explicit approval
acceptance_criteria: Mandate file defines capital base, per-trade caps, daily/weekly loss limits, margin/options boundaries, and kill switch.
Consent artifact exists before live order code can submit.
No live order path can bypass guard, review, reconciliation, and audit ledger.
anti_slop_rules: Do not describe paper/staged trading as live readiness.
Do not clear circuit breaker or trading halt inside the build without decision evidence.
Do not claim options support until Robinhood tool discovery proves it.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Ratatosk executor tests need cleanup around live gates and submitted order position recording.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Gemini and non-Robinhood broker support remain out of v0.1.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Live trading remains blocked while the active Ratatosk circuit-breaker/trading halt is present, even though the testing-scope decision is resolved.

compatibility_contract.backward: Keep current fail-closed live trading behavior.
compatibility_contract.forward: Mandate can later widen after measured performance and explicit approval.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Start with read-only and stage-only finance canaries, then approval-only live canary.

implementation_notes: Use Ratatosk v0.1 Robinhood brief as research input but validate against current local ADLC schema.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: live order blocked without consent or live gates

verification_spec.scope_note: Decision must not be inferred from tests passing.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture mandate, consent, guard, circuit breaker, and live validator outputs.
definition_of_done: Live finance decision resolved before broker order execution is enabled.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Broker order submitted without written mandate and consent.
CRITICAL: Margin/options trade bypasses caps or eligibility checks.

8_task_tickets.title: Implement Torben submanager capability contracts
8_task_tickets.objective: Create one contract layer for hidden EA, GTM/Magnus, and Finance/Ratatosk capabilities so Torben can route evidence, actions, approvals, health, and investigations consistently.

decision_contract.resolution: No Type 1 decision required for read/stage/health contracts.

acceptance_criteria: Torben can list EA, GTM, and Finance backend health in one artifact.
Every backend candidate action includes scope, handle prefix, evidence ids, risk class, allowed next actions, and mutation status.
Duplicate/no-op backend runs stay silent.
anti_slop_rules: Do not create three separate action ledgers.
Do not let submanager scripts print raw reports directly to Signal.
Do not store secrets or raw OAuth tokens in the contract artifacts.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Current GTM and EA artifacts have adjacent but not fully unified shapes.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Historical artifacts do not need migration unless they are still active.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: New contracts can wrap old artifacts with adapters.

compatibility_contract.backward: Current Torben scripts and tests keep passing.
compatibility_contract.forward: Future hidden submanagers can plug into the same contract.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Add contract adapters before changing cron behavior.

implementation_notes: Prefer hermes_cli/signal_coo modules over profile-local one-off logic.
Integrate with live-profile verifier and investigation request state.

verification_spec.scope_note: Contract tests should not need network access.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture test output and live-profile verifier output.
definition_of_done: EA, GTM, and Finance can be represented in one Torben-owned capability registry.
failure_modes: HIGH: Hidden capability emits unstructured text that bypasses Torben policy.

8_task_tickets.title: Finish Magnus as hidden GTM content intelligence submanager
8_task_tickets.objective: Complete the Torben-owned GTM loop so Magnus/Grok source intelligence, X algorithm pressure tests, article packages, visuals, and reply recommendations operate through Signal handles with no unattended public action.

decision_contract.resolution: Read/stage GTM intelligence and drafts can proceed without public mutation enablement.

scope: Refresh Magnus GTM sources from Torben
Use Grok/xAI and X search for GTM judgment
Use xai-org/x-algorithm as a pressure-test lens
Stage longform, LinkedIn, X post, X thread, reply, and visual packages
acceptance_criteria: Torben GTM radar can refresh sources and emit 1-3 actionable findings with links and handles.
GTM engagement radar can stage reply recommendations with context, exact draft, and source evidence.
Content packages include thesis, source notes, platform-specific copy, visual plan, X algorithm pressure-test, and approval actions.
anti_slop_rules: Do not open articles with a paper summary unless requested.
Do not emit draft in backlog without useful summary and next action.
Do not claim X algorithm guarantees private ranking outcomes.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Old magnus profile cron state is empty; Torben is the active scheduler.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Legacy Magnus-local queues can remain as backend caches if Torben owns delivery.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: All visible GTM output must pass through Torben action handles.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing torben-gtm-radar and torben-gtm-engagement-radar keep their schedules and no-agent delivery.
compatibility_contract.forward: Public-write adapters can attach after TBC-DECIDE-PUBLIC-MUTATION resolves.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Start with preview and local package canaries before Signal delivery.

implementation_notes: Use Grok for Magnus review and X integration; use Codex/OpenAI only for longform/code work if explicitly needed.
Use EA newsletter extraction as an additional GTM source but keep realtime email separate from GTM radar.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: GTM package or reply router not fully Torben-owned

verification_spec.scope_note: Network canaries run separately from deterministic unit tests.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture preview output, Signal-safe text, and public_actions_taken=0.
definition_of_done: Torben can stage GTM intelligence and content packages through Signal with no public mutation.
failure_modes: MEDIUM: GTM radar is technically running but produces low-signal or duplicate findings.
HIGH: Public write adapter is reachable before approval.

8_task_tickets.title: Finish EA mutation backends behind Torben approvals
8_task_tickets.objective: Complete approval-gated Gmail send, Gmail cleanup, and real calendar edit/delete backends while preserving current auto busy-block calendar alignment and no-noise realtime triage.

decision_contract.resolution: Guarded stage/apply code can be implemented without enabling broad autonomy.

scope: Relationship learning and action memory integration
acceptance_criteria: Every staged email response includes thread context and what the draft is trying to say.
Cleanup recommendations can be approved by handle and apply only guarded trash/archive actions.
Calendar mutation proposals include source event, target calendar, rollback metadata, and approval handle.
anti_slop_rules: Do not classify email importance from body keywords alone.
Do not permanently delete Gmail messages.
Do not edit attendee-bearing events without explicit approval and audit.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Realtime triage and weekly hygiene already exist but not all send/edit paths exist.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Later autonomy can be learned from 30 days of approvals.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Approval-only apply paths prevent accidental mutation.

compatibility_contract.backward: Keep Gmail Pub/Sub realtime, morning brief, calendar alignment, and meeting prep behavior.
compatibility_contract.forward: Same action-ledger resolver can later support limited autonomy.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Canary each Gmail/calendar mutation class on a synthetic or explicitly approved target first.

implementation_notes: Google Gmail API send/trash/modify and Calendar events insert/patch/delete are the provider boundaries.
Calendar busy-block auto-create remains narrowly allowed; real event edit/delete stays approval-only.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: EA mutation resolver missing approval guard or context

verification_spec.scope_note: Live Google write canaries require explicit approved handles.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture synthetic apply tests and one approved live canary per mutation class before enabling.
definition_of_done: Torben can stage and, after approval, apply EA mutations with audit and rollback/reconciliation data.
failure_modes: HIGH: Gmail cleanup applies from weekly cron without handle approval.
HIGH: Email send loses thread context and sends generic copy.

8_task_tickets.title: Finish Ratatosk as hidden Finance submanager in stage-only mode
8_task_tickets.objective: Wire Ratatosk finance research, Robinhood candidate staging, Monarch personal finance insight, mandate validation, and fail-closed live order checks behind Torben FIN handles without submitting live orders.

decision_contract.resolution: Stage-only finance capability remains the default. The live finance decision is resolved for a tiny testing canary, but the active Ratatosk circuit-breaker/trading halt still blocks broker execution.

scope: Create or emulate hidden Ratatosk submanager under Torben
Robinhood MCP discovery and read/candidate staging
Monarch MCP read insights and approval-gated recommendations
FIN handles and Signal approval packets
out_of_scope: Clearing circuit breaker or trading halt
acceptance_criteria: Torben can emit FIN handles for research, watchlist, candidate trades, risk reviews, and Monarch finance recommendations.
Robinhood candidate staging proves research_signal_can_trade_directly=false.
Live order attempts remain blocked without mandate, consent, live env gates, and guard pass.
No broker order or Monarch mutation occurs during scheduled stage-only jobs.
anti_slop_rules: Do not say ROI is proven from staged candidates.
Do not treat account read access as trade permission.
Do not bypass circuit breaker or trading halt in tests or live profile setup.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Ratatosk executor tests need live-gate and position-recording cleanup.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Live trading and options execution wait for Type-1 decision.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Stage-only mode preserves finance signal without capital mutation.

compatibility_contract.backward: Keep current Ratatosk fail-closed behavior and v0.1 validator.
compatibility_contract.forward: Live finance task can enable orders after mandate and consent.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Start as Torben FIN handles, then decide whether to create a hidden Hermes ratatosk profile.

implementation_notes: Use Robinhood Agentic MCP for brokerage path and Monarch MCP for personal finance path.
Stage-only crons mint one bounded LLM run per tick and never expose broker order tools to the LLM.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: finance path can stage candidates but live order is blocked

verification_spec.scope_note: Live MCP canaries must run read-only or stage-only unless Type-1 decision resolves.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture validator output, circuit breaker state, trading halt state, and FIN action ledger entries.
definition_of_done: Torben can surface Finance/Ratatosk stage-only signals through FIN handles with live orders blocked.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Stage-only finance job places a broker order.
HIGH: Candidate lacks risk/reward, source evidence, or guard decision.

8_task_tickets.title: Enable approval-gated public and personal mutation adapters
8_task_tickets.objective: After the public mutation decision resolves, connect approved Torben handles to X/LinkedIn posting, Gmail send, Gmail cleanup, and real calendar edit/delete adapters with provider verification and audit.

decision_contract.resolution: Implementation depends on TBC-DECIDE-PUBLIC-MUTATION being resolved before execution.

scope: X API or browser/Postiz approval-gated publish path
LinkedIn Posts API or browser/Postiz approval-gated publish path
Gmail send and cleanup apply path
acceptance_criteria: Approved handle resolves to exact draft/source/evidence before mutation.
Provider response is stored with mutation id, timestamp, target account, and rollback/reconciliation status.
Failed provider calls create one actionable failure and no duplicate spam.
anti_slop_rules: Do not post if the draft has changed since approval.
Do not use browser automation when API path is available and authorized unless explicitly selected.
Do not hide platform policy or rate-limit failures.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: OAuth/API access and app approval may vary by platform.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Approval-gated public write canary runs one item at a time.

compatibility_contract.backward: Draft/source/hold replies keep working even if publish adapters are unavailable.
compatibility_contract.forward: Adapters can swap API, Postiz, or browser backend without changing Torben handle semantics.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Enable one platform/action at a time with canary evidence.

implementation_notes: X API create post requires user-context write capability.
LinkedIn Posts API requires appropriate member or organization author permissions.
Gmail send and Calendar mutation use already authorized Google account scopes only after approval.

primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: approved handle cannot reach guarded provider adapter

verification_spec.scope_note: Live platform canaries require explicit approved handles.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture provider response, audit ledger entry, and rollback/reconciliation metadata.
definition_of_done: One approved canary per enabled mutation class succeeds or fails safely with audit evidence.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Public post or email sends without current approved handle.
HIGH: Provider write succeeds but audit record is missing.

8_task_tickets.title: Enable approval-gated live finance execution
8_task_tickets.objective: After the live finance decision resolves, enable Ratatosk/Torben live Robinhood and Monarch mutations under mandate, consent, hard limits, kill switch, and reconciliation.

decision_contract.resolution: Implementation depends on TBC-DECIDE-LIVE-FINANCE being resolved before execution.

scope: Robinhood options discovery and long-call/put approval path
acceptance_criteria: No live order can submit unless mandate, consent, live env gates, kill switch, and pretrade guard pass.
Options execution is enabled only if Robinhood discovery proves account/tool support and contract filters pass.
Every order has pretrade decision, provider response, fill/reject status, position reconciliation, PnL attribution, and halt-on-divergence.
anti_slop_rules: Do not enable margin borrowing by implication.
Do not infer options support from account hope; discover tools and approval state.
Do not mark live finance ready while executor tests fail.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Executor position-recording tests currently fail for submitted/unfilled mocked orders.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Gemini and broader brokerage support remain out of scope.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Fail-closed live gates remain default until tests and canaries pass.

compatibility_contract.backward: Stage-only finance remains available if live canary fails.
compatibility_contract.forward: Performance optimizer can change rankings but not widen risk limits.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Use one tiny approved live canary before scheduled live trading.

implementation_notes: Fix executor tests before enabling live order canary.
Use Robinhood Agentic MCP account separation and MCP tool discovery as hard gates.

primary_verifier.target: cd /Users/ericfreeman/ratatosk && uv run python -m pytest tests/test_robinhood_v01 tests/test_engine/test_executor_robinhood.py -q
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: executor live gates or position reconciliation fail

verification_spec.scope_note: Live canary itself is separate and requires explicit approval.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture mandate, consent, unit tests, dry-run guard decision, live canary provider response, and reconciliation report.
definition_of_done: Approval-gated live finance path is proven by tests and one approved canary, or remains explicitly blocked with reason.
failure_modes: CRITICAL: Live order submitted with missing consent.
CRITICAL: Reconciliation mismatch does not halt future orders.

8_task_tickets.title: Validate Torben backend capability completion end to end
8_task_tickets.objective: Prove Torben can coordinate EA, GTM, and Finance submanagers with no-noise background behavior, actionable Signal output, approval-gated mutations, live-profile investigation, and fail-closed finance.
scope: GTM radar, engagement, content package, and public-write blocked canaries
Finance stage-only and live-order blocked canaries
acceptance_criteria: Torben live-profile verifier passes.
Healthy background ticks with no actionable items stay silent.
Synthetic failure wakes one LLM investigation and suppresses duplicate failures.
GTM can stage content package and keep public_actions_taken=0 without approval.
EA can stage and apply only approved synthetic or explicitly approved mutations.
Finance can stage candidates and prove live orders are blocked without mandate/consent/live gates.
anti_slop_rules: Do not accept green unit tests without live profile proof.
Do not hide failed canaries behind silence.
Do not run live mutations as part of validation unless explicitly approved.

tech_debt_boundaries.prerequisite_debt: Some live provider auth and external app permissions may require manual approval.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferred_debt: Longer-term autonomy tuning remains a post-canary phase.
tech_debt_boundaries.deferral_safety: Validation reports remaining provider and safety blockers instead of bypassing them.

compatibility_contract.backward: Existing Torben production jobs remain active and no noisier than before.
compatibility_contract.forward: Validation becomes the reusable preflight before expanding autonomy.
compatibility_contract.migration_or_rollout: Run validation before enabling each mutation class.

implementation_notes: Use current Torben live-profile verification and investigation job as the operational health base.
Report canary evidence in one closeout artifact.

primary_verifier.target: cd /Users/ericfreeman/.hermes/hermes-agent && venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_signal_coo.py tests/test_signal_coo_gmail_realtime.py tests/test_signal_coo_live_profile_verify.py tests/test_torben_gtm_engagement_radar.py tests/gateway/test_torben_gtm_reply_router.py -q

secondary_verifiers.expected_failure_mode: finance stage-only validator missing or unsafe

verification_spec.scope_note: Live external canaries require explicit approval and should be reported separately.

evidence_responsibilities: Capture pytest output, live cron list, live-profile verifier JSON, GTM preview output, Gmail realtime latest, and Ratatosk validator output.
definition_of_done: A single validation closeout proves Torben backend capability completion or lists exact remaining blockers.
failure_modes: HIGH: Validation passes while live profile is degraded.
HIGH: A no-op background job sends Signal noise.

9_risks: Single-interface convenience can hide dangerous external mutation boundaries unless action classes stay explicit.
Public platform APIs and app permissions may block direct X/LinkedIn posting even if local code is ready.
Robinhood Agentic MCP support and options/margin availability must be discovered live and may not match desired scope.
Monarch MCP may be read-heavy first; write actions need separate approval and provider capability validation.
LLM-driven crons can become noisy or expensive without wake gates, dedupe, and candidate caps.
Live finance canary execution remains blocked by the active Ratatosk circuit-breaker/trading halt until Eric separately approves clearing it with current risk evidence.
sections.10_rollout: Roll out in four stages. Stage 1: contract and health only, no new mutations. Stage 2: GTM and EA stage/apply canaries for approved handles, still no public posting without approval. Stage 3: Finance stage-only canaries and Ratatosk profile/submanager wiring, with live orders blocked. Stage 4: after Type-1 decisions, one approved canary per mutation class, then scheduled operation with live-profile verifier and investigation loop watching for drift.
11_validation_plan: Validate this ADLC brief with the local ADLC CLI.
Run Torben unit and gateway tests for Signal action ledger, Gmail realtime, live-profile verification, GTM radar, and GTM reply routing.
Run Torben live-profile verifier and confirm status=pass.
Run GTM preview and confirm Grok/X algorithm lens, handles, useful summaries, public_actions_taken=0.
Run Gmail realtime latest check and confirm no stale Pub/Sub failures.
Run Ratatosk Robinhood v0.1 validator and confirm stage allowed, live order blocked without mandate/consent/gates.
Run synthetic live-profile failure and confirm one investigation request plus duplicate suppression.
Run approved live canaries only after the relevant Type-1 decision is resolved.

12_skill_trigger_configuration.mode: draft/source/hold first, publish only after approval

13_comprehension_context.operator_model: Torben is Eric-facing. Submanagers are hidden capability modules.
13_comprehension_context.core_rule: Crons wake bounded LLM workflows; deterministic code collects evidence and enforces policy; neither static cron nor LLM output may mutate externally without a guard and approval.

required_checks: No duplicate Signal reports on success/no-op
No external mutation without action-ledger approval
No broker order tools exposed to LLM cron analysis
No raw OAuth token or secret values in artifacts
